deep_dive.title: Recursive Harness Self-Improvement
deep_dive.abstract: Under model--harness co-evolution, harnesses are not merely inference-time scaffolds but data-generating components whose execution traces can shape future foundation models. This motivates harness-in-the-loop learning: optimizing harnesses for both immediate agent performance and the quality of traces used for future model training. However, continually updating provider-built scaffolds is costly and labor-intensive. We therefore investigate whether optimizing user-constructed harnesses in a task-specific manner can improve execution-trace quality while remaining computationally lightweight and requiring only a few update iterations. To this end, we introduce Recursive Harness Self-Improvement (RHI), which represents the harness as a prompt-level specification of the agent loop and iteratively refines it using pairwise feedback over its own revision history. Across 30 synthetic machine-learning research tasks spanning quantitative finance, robotics, and pharmacy, a few RHI iterations suffice to substantially raise the performance ceiling of low-reasoning-effort agents, exceeding the corresponding maximum-reasoning-effort setting while reducing inference cost by up to 60%. We show that these gains arise primarily from improved task-specific context management through more effective inter-agent information flow rather than longer reasoning traces. Finally, we formalize this behavior as an information-theoretic hypothesis for RHI's implicit optimization objective, suggesting RHI as a practical algorithm for continual learning within the paradigm of model--harness co-evolution.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: recursive harness self-improvement iteratively improves agent loops
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 递归驾驭自我改进，迭代优化代理循环
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 递归驾驭自我改进，迭代优化代理循环
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 递归驾驭自我改进，迭代优化代理循环
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Recursive Harness Self-Improvement (RHI) treats the harness as a prompt-level specification and iteratively optimizes it to improve both agent performance and execution trace quality for future model training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 在当前模型-驾驭协同进化中，驾驭不仅是推理支架，还是数据生成组件。RHI将驾驭表示为提示级规格，通过少量迭代优化用户构建的驾驭，以提升执行轨迹质量和代理性能。实验表明该方法轻量且有效，实现了代理的自我改进。

deep_dive.title: From Memory to Skills: Evidence-Grounded Co-Evolution Governance for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Existing memory systems for long-horizon LLM agents often retrieve prior traces as passive context rather than converting them into executable capabilities. In this paper, we propose MSCE, a training-free Memory--Skill Co-Evolution framework that organizes agent experience into grounded step traces, reusable procedural policies, and declarative environmental cognition. MSCE crystallizes evidence-backed L2 policies with positive estimated gain into callable skills that retain evidence links, applicability boundaries, decision guidance, verification rules, and reliability estimates. It further introduces reflection-weighted value backfilling, which propagates sparse terminal feedback through dense local self-reflections to produce evidence-calibrated trace values for governing memory and skill evolution. Experiments on EvoAgentBench and LoCoMo demonstrate that MSCE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art skill-augmented and memory-driven agent baselines, exhibiting strong cross-domain transferability and lifelong-evolution capabilities.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 长程LLM智能体记忆-技能共演化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 长程LLM智能体记忆-技能共演化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 长程LLM智能体记忆-技能共演化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MSCE, a training-free framework that converts long-horizon agent experience into callable skills with evidence links, using reflection-weighted value backfilling to improve long-horizon task performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有长程LLM智能体的记忆系统只能检索过去轨迹作为被动上下文，无法将其转化为可执行的技能。本文提出MSCE，一个无需训练的记忆-技能共演化框架，将智能体经验组织为带证据的步骤轨迹、可复用的程序化策略和声明式环境认知，并通过反射加权值回填机制将稀疏的终局反馈通过局部自反思传播。实验表明，该方法在多个长程任务上显著提升了成功率，为构建能自主进化的长程智能体提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有长程LLM智能体的记忆系统只能检索过去轨迹作为被动上下文，无法将其转化为可执行的技能。本文提出MSCE，一个无需训练的记忆-技能共演化框架，将智能体经验组织为带证据的步骤轨迹、可复用的程序化策略和声明式环境认知，并通过反射加权值回填机制将稀疏的终局反馈通过局部自反思传播。实验表明，该方法在多个长程任务上显著提升了成功率，为构建能自主进化的长程智能体提供了新途径。

deep_dive.title: Cura 1T: Specialized Model for Agentic Healthcare
deep_dive.abstract: Healthcare spans high-stakes communication, expert reasoning, and workflow execution, yet specialized LLMs that cover these use cases together remain limited. A healthcare model must handle patient consultation, clinical reasoning over text and images, interactive diagnosis, and electronic health record (EHR) tool use. These capabilities fail in different ways, and a narrow update for one task can degrade another. We present Cura 1T, a healthcare-specialized LLM trained through a human-gated self-evolution loop. In each evolution round, a training agent plans a target capability, trains the model, evaluates benchmark trajectories, and refines the data mixture from observed failures. This data-centered loop improves the model through targeted synthetic and curated examples rather than a single generic medical-data update. Across the healthcare evaluation suite, Cura 1T ranks at or near the top among frontier baselines, while remaining competitive on out-of-domain reasoning and agentic benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: human-gated self-evolution loop for improving agent capabilities
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 人类门控的自演化循环提升智能体能力
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 人类门控的自演化循环提升智能体能力
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 人类门控的自演化循环提升智能体能力
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Cura 1T uses a self-evolution loop with human gating to iteratively improve a healthcare LLM through plan-train-evaluate-refine cycles.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 医疗领域需要专业LLM同时处理咨询、推理和工具使用，但单一模型更新容易导致能力退化。本文提出Cura 1T，通过人类门控的自演化循环：训练智能体规划目标能力、训练模型、评估基准轨迹并从失败中调整数据配比。该数据驱动的循环不断改进模型，在多项医疗任务上达到最优性能，展示了自改进智能体在特定领域的潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 医疗领域需要专业LLM同时处理咨询、推理和工具使用，但单一模型更新容易导致能力退化。本文提出Cura 1T，通过人类门控的自演化循环：训练智能体规划目标能力、训练模型、评估基准轨迹并从失败中调整数据配比。该数据驱动的循环不断改进模型，在多项医疗任务上达到最优性能，展示了自改进智能体在特定领域的潜力。

deep_dive.title: Process Reward Informed Tree Rollout for Effective Multi-Turn RL
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key approach for training LLM agents, yet popular methods such as GRPO/RLOO rely on multiple independently sampled complete trajectories for advantage estimation. In long-horizon agentic tasks, such a uniform rollout strategy can waste budget on uninformative dead-end attempts, while promising intermediate states do not receive sufficient exploration. The multi-turn structure of agentic trajectories, with interleaved actions and observations, naturally supports organizing a trajectory group as a tree, where each turn serves as a decision point for exploration. This perspective reframes effective exploration as the problem of deciding where to branch. We propose Process-Scorer Guided Adaptive Tree Rollout (PATR), a quality-aware rollout framework for multi-turn agent RL. PATR uses task-appropriate process feedback to score partial trajectories, selectively branches from promising states, reuses shared prefixes, and conservatively stops degenerate paths to reduce wasted sampling. The resulting rollout groups remain compatible with standard policy optimization while providing more efficient exploration under the same training budget. We evaluate PATR on FrozenLake and the challenging SWE-Bench, which is largely unexplored by prior tree-rollout agent RL methods. Experiments show that PATR improves performance by up to +5.0 points on SWE-Bench and +9.3 points on FrozenLake, highlighting process-guided tree rollouts as an effective strategy for scalable multi-turn RL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: tree rollout for long-horizon agentic RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向长程智能体强化学习的树展开
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向长程智能体强化学习的树展开
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向长程智能体强化学习的树展开
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PATR uses process-supervised guidance for adaptive tree rollout to improve exploration efficiency in long-horizon multi-turn RL tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长程智能体任务中，传统RL方法均匀采样完整轨迹浪费预算。本文提出过程奖励引导的适应性树展开（PATR），将多轮轨迹组织为树结构，通过过程评分器决定何处分支，高效探索有希望的状态。实验表明，在多个长程智能体任务中，PATR显著提升了样本效率和任务成功率。

deep_dive.title: ProEvent: An Event-centric Benchmark for Proactive Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Proactive agents are expected to anticipate user needs and provide autonomous assistance by perceiving environmental context without explicit instructions. A fundamental capability of such agents is to identify and track users' upcoming events, enabling continuous and event-specific assistance. For example, by recording the time and location of a planned hike, an agent can deliver weather reminders in advance or provide navigation support before departure. However, existing works on proactive agents largely overlook event-centric assistance, and the open-ended nature of proactive assistance poses challenges for reliable evaluation.   To bridge these gaps, we introduce ProEvent, the first event-centric benchmark designed to assess an agent's ability to proactively maintain a user's timetable based on ongoing instant messaging chats. ProEvent provides synthesized yet realistic chats that consider the dynamic interaction among users, concurrent chat threads, and noise in the real world, and evaluates proactive agents on response timing, single-step response correctness, and multi-step response correctness. Experiments on eight LLMs and pipelines reveal that current agents frequently overact and struggle with event cancellation. Notably, even GPT-5.1 only reacts correctly in 26.7% of scenarios. Further qualitative analysis reveals fundamental limitations of current LLMs as proactive agents, particularly in detecting implicit events and reasoning from the user's first-person perspective.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: event-centric benchmark for proactive agents with long-horizon event tracking
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 以事件为中心的主动代理基准，涉及长时程事件跟踪
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 以事件为中心的主动代理基准，涉及长时程事件跟踪
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 以事件为中心的主动代理基准，涉及长时程事件跟踪
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ProEvent benchmarks proactive agents on event-centric tasks requiring anticipation and long-horizon assistance, evaluating their ability to track and act on future events.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有主动代理研究忽视事件中心辅助，且开放式评估困难。ProEvent提出首个以事件为中心的基准，测试代理感知环境、识别用户未来事件并提供持续辅助的能力。实验揭示当前代理在长期事件跟踪上的显著不足。该基准为开发长时程规划代理提供了关键评估工具。

deep_dive.title: RoboHarness: Memory-Driven Orchestration of Heterogeneous Robot Policies for Long-Horizon Planning
deep_dive.abstract: Long-horizon robotic tasks require diverse capabilities that no single policy can reliably provide. Heterogeneous policies offer complementary strengths, but orchestrating them requires reasoning over uncertain capability boundaries and cross-policy distribution mismatch, which are largely overlooked by existing planning methods built on homogeneous, predefined skills with fixed applicability. We propose RoboHarness, a unified framework that encapsulates independently developed robot control systems as reusable agentic skills. Although instantiated in this work with VLAs, RL policies, and task-and-motion planning (TAMP) systems, RoboHarness is designed as a general framework compatible with a broader range of robot policies, such as navigation policies, model predictive controllers, and world-action models. RoboHarness uses multi-modal execution memory and online evidence to characterize policy capability boundaries for capability-aware decomposition and routing. To stabilize policy handoffs, its Memory Bridge retrieves execution trajectories associated with the next policy, estimates its in-distribution state region, and guides the robot toward that region without joint policy retraining. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks, 500 customized tasks, and 135 real-robot experiments demonstrate effective capability-aware routing and stable policy orchestration, yielding substantial improvements in zero-shot long-horizon planning and out-of-distribution robustness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: memory-driven orchestration for long-horizon robot planning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 记忆驱动的异构机器人策略编排用于长视界规划
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 记忆驱动的异构机器人策略编排用于长视界规划
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 记忆驱动的异构机器人策略编排用于长视界规划
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RoboHarness for orchestrating heterogeneous robot policies using memory to enable long-horizon planning.

deep_dive.title: VDAR-Router: Adaptive LLMs Routing via Verbalized Query Difficulty Analysis Retrieval
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models are increasingly used in practical systems, making efficient model selection important for reducing deployment cost. LLM routing has emerged as a practical solution for allocating each input query to an appropriate model under a desired cost-performance trade-off. Existing routing methods often estimate model suitability from the surface semantics or embedding similarity of the input query. However, such methods may ignore the underlying difficulty of a query, leading to suboptimal routing decisions. To address the challenge, we propose VDAR-Router, a difficulty-aware retrieval-based routing framework. For each input query, VDAR-Router first generates an explicit difficulty analysis. It then retrieves historical examples with similar difficulty profiles. Based on the retrieved records, it estimates candidate model suitability and selects the model using a reward function that considers both performance and cost. Experiments on three datasets show that VDAR-Router consistently achieves better cost-performance trade-offs than existing baselines. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of difficulty-aware retrieval for training-free LLM routing. Case studies further show that explicit query analysis helps retrieve more relevant examples and supports more reliable routing decisions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a difficulty-aware routing framework for LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向LLM推理的基于难度分析的路由框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出面向LLM推理的基于难度分析的路由框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出面向LLM推理的基于难度分析的路由框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VDAR-Router is a difficulty-aware retrieval-based routing framework that first generates explicit difficulty analysis for each query, then retrieves historical examples to select the optimal LLM, improving cost-performance trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM路由方法多基于表面语义或嵌入相似性，忽略查询内在难度。VDAR-Router首先生成显式难度分析，再检索历史样例实现自适应路由。在多个基准上，该方法在保持性能的同时显著降低推理成本，展示了难度感知路由的实用价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM路由方法多基于表面语义或嵌入相似性，忽略查询内在难度。VDAR-Router首先生成显式难度分析，再检索历史样例实现自适应路由。在多个基准上，该方法在保持性能的同时显著降低推理成本，展示了难度感知路由的实用价值。

deep_dive.title: FlashRT: Agent Harness for Guiding Agents to Deploy Real-Time Multimodal Applications
deep_dive.abstract: Real-time multimodal applications, including voice agents and interactive video generation, compose heterogeneous models into pipelines whose efficient deployment requires application-specific decisions about placement, streaming, and intra-model parallelism. Existing serving systems and auto-parallelism compilers commit to limited transformations and fixed workload assumptions, so achieving high performance on a new application requires hand-crafting an efficient implementation. We present FlashRT, an agent harness that guides coding agents to lift simple developer-written reference implementations into optimized multi-GPU deployments that flexibly weigh target metrics like latency and throughput. Using a new chain-of-program paradigm, FlashRT directs a generic coding agent through a multi-pass transformation process where an agent transforms the reference into an intermediate representation (IR) to capture data dependencies and persistent-state scopes, validates this IR via a sequential interpreter, and performs static analyses to identify candidate transformations. Then, the agent iteratively implements, verifies, and benchmarks each candidate under a measurement-gated optimization loop to produce effective deployments that span different hardware budgets. Across various applications, including video world models and multimodal LLMs, FlashRT converts reference implementations into highly efficient deployments, delivering up to ~70x latency reduction and 2.8x throughput improvement on NVIDIA B200 GPUs. On AMD MI355X GPUs, FlashRT matches the peak latency reduction while increasing peak throughput improvement to 3.6x, demonstrating that agent-driven optimization can be more scalable on platforms with less mature expert optimization. In fact, for Qwen3-Omni text-to-audio inference, FlashRT reduces response latency by 65% compared to the expert vLLM-Omni implementation on AMD MI355X.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: agent harness for guiding agent deployment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于指导智能体部署的智能体缰绳
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于指导智能体部署的智能体缰绳
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于指导智能体部署的智能体缰绳
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FlashRT uses a chain-of-program paradigm to guide coding agents in optimizing deployment of real-time multimodal applications, acting as an agent harness for controlling agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 实时多模态应用的部署需要针对性的并行化和流式策略，现有系统难以自动适应。本文提出FlashRT，一个智能体缰绳框架，通过链式编程范式引导编码智能体将简单的参考实现自动优化为高性能多GPU部署。实验表明，FlashRT能显著降低部署延迟并提高吞吐量，为动态环境下智能体控制与部署提供了有效工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 实时多模态应用的部署需要针对性的并行化和流式策略，现有系统难以自动适应。本文提出FlashRT，一个智能体缰绳框架，通过链式编程范式引导编码智能体将简单的参考实现自动优化为高性能多GPU部署。实验表明，FlashRT能显著降低部署延迟并提高吞吐量，为动态环境下智能体控制与部署提供了有效工具。

deep_dive.title: Operational Hallucination and Safety Drift in AI Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) serving as planners in tool-using autonomous agents introduce dynamic reliability risks in multi-turn execution. While single-turn safety mechanisms are relatively mature, extended interactions reveal structural vulnerabilities where initial alignment degrades over time. This paper empirically characterizes two observed failure modes across multiple state-of-the-art LLMs: Safety Drift, the gradual erosion of declared safety intent leading to constraint-violating actions (e.g., textual refusal followed by reconnaissance and unsafe execution), and Operational Hallucination, persistent repetitive tool calls indicative of flawed state perception (e.g., livelocks even in legitimate tasks). Through controlled multi-turn evaluation on high-stakes ethical dilemmas, malicious requests, and benign controls, we quantify these phenomena using declaration-action gap and livelock metrics, demonstrating their cross-model prevalence under direct execution protocols. Root-cause analysis attributes the instabilities to the decoupling of reasoning context from execution state in current agent loops. We propose an Action-Aware Supervision Layer - a lightweight, plug-and-play architectural blueprint incorporating intent-action consistency checks, runtime state tracking, and forced termination primitives. Post-hoc simulation on captured failure trajectories shows the layer can intercept observed violations without false positives on benign cases. This work advances agent reliability by shifting focus from linguistic safeguards to enforceable architectural mechanisms for responsible agentic AI.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Characterizes safety drift and operational hallucination in long-horizon agent interactions, directly relevant to agents operating over long horizons.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 刻画了长时域智能体交互中的安全漂移与操作性幻觉，直接相关长时域智能体。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 刻画了长时域智能体交互中的安全漂移与操作性幻觉，直接相关长时域智能体。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 刻画了长时域智能体交互中的安全漂移与操作性幻觉，直接相关长时域智能体。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper empirically identifies Safety Drift and Operational Hallucination as failure modes in LLM agents during extended multi-turn execution, highlighting reliability risks in long-horizon operation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大规模语言模型作为规划器在多轮工具使用中会出现动态可靠性风险。本文通过受控实验刻画了两种失效模式：安全漂移（初始对齐随时间退化）和操作性幻觉（重复工具调用）。结果表明，即使初始安全机制完善，长期交互中仍会出现约束违规行为，对部署高可靠性智能体构成挑战。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大规模语言模型作为规划器在多轮工具使用中会出现动态可靠性风险。本文通过受控实验刻画了两种失效模式：安全漂移（初始对齐随时间退化）和操作性幻觉（重复工具调用）。结果表明，即使初始安全机制完善，长期交互中仍会出现约束违规行为，对部署高可靠性智能体构成挑战。

quick_skim.title: ToolVerse: Unlocking Massive Environments and Long-Horizon Tasks for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: While LLM agents demonstrate strong reasoning abilities in compact and well-defined scenarios, they struggle to maintain robustness and effectiveness when faced with large-scale, diverse, and dynamic real-world environments that demand seamless tool integration. To address this gap, we introduce ToolVerse, a comprehensive framework that scales up agentic RL environments and enables agents to perform complex long-horizon reasoning in Tool-Integrated Reasoning (TIR) tasks. First, ToolVerse automatically builds the massive executable agent training environments from nearly 400 real-world Model Context Protocols (MCPs) that contain about 4500 tools. Second, we propose a task design strategy based on a tool dependency graph, utilizing Dynamic Unlocking Sampling Algorithm to generate long-horizon tasks, and produce GUST (Graph Unlocking Sampling Tasks) dataset. Third, to alleviate the credit assigment problem in long-horizon agentic RL, we propose a fine-grained Turn-Aware Relative Advantage algorithm. We conduct extensive Agentic RL training using ToolVerse and evaluate our framework on serveral agentic benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework significantly strengthens LLMs' capabilities in long-horizon tool use, achieving a marked performance boost and showcasing robust reasoning within dynamic environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Provides massive environments and long-horizon tasks for agentic RL, directly relevant to agents operating over long horizons.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为智能体强化学习提供大规模环境和长时域任务，直接相关长时域智能体。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为智能体强化学习提供大规模环境和长时域任务，直接相关长时域智能体。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为智能体强化学习提供大规模环境和长时域任务，直接相关长时域智能体。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ToolVerse automatically builds massive executable agent training environments from 400 real-world MCPs with 4500 tools, enabling long-horizon reasoning in Tool-Integrated Reasoning tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM智能体在大规模动态环境中表现不佳，缺乏工具集成。ToolVerse从近400个真实MCP自动构建可执行训练环境，包含约4500个工具，并基于工具依赖图设计任务，使智能体能够进行复杂的长时域工具集成推理。实验证明该方法显著提升了智能体在复杂现实场景中的鲁棒性和有效性。

quick_skim.title: ReasFlow: Assisting Reasoning-Centric Scientific Discovery in Applied Mathematics via a Knowledge-Based Multi-Agent System
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in Large Language Models have fueled autonomous AI agents capable of tackling complex scientific tasks, yet existing automated research systems remain predominantly focused on empirically driven domains with quantitative benchmarks, leaving theory-driven discovery, particularly in mathematically grounded disciplines requiring rigorous proofs and synthesis of domain knowledge, largely underexplored. Key challenges include the difficulty of verifying theoretical reasoning at scale, insufficient reasoning ability for autonomous frontier exploration, and a scarcity of procedural heuristics in the literature. We introduce ReasFlow, an end-to-end autonomous agent system for reasoning-centric scientific discovery that operationalizes a collaborative paradigm where the human expert acts as Principal Investigator while the agent executes rigorous derivations as a capable graduate student. ReasFlow incorporates (i) a robust internal verification loop that audits logical coherence and corrects fundamental errors prior to human inspection, and (ii) an automated knowledge retrieval and self-improvement mechanism that proactively surfaces both declarative facts and overlooked procedural heuristics, substantially reducing expert intervention. The system unifies literature synthesis, algorithm design, theorem proving, experimentation, and manuscript preparation in a single system. Deployed to autonomously generate five complete research papers with rigorous theoretical and empirical content from minimal prompts, ReasFlow consistently achieves the highest evaluation scores among state-of-the-art open-access baselines under a curated LLM-based review rubric. ReasFlow is publicly accessible via the ReasLab platform, providing a collaborative workspace for AI-assisted theoretical research. Github repo: https://github.com/ReasLab/ReasFlow.git.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: autonomous agent system for reasoning-centric scientific discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自主智能体系统用于以推理为中心的科学发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自主智能体系统用于以推理为中心的科学发现

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ReasFlow is an end-to-end autonomous agent system for reasoning-centric scientific discovery in applied mathematics, using a knowledge-based multi-agent approach to tackle theory-driven discovery.

quick_skim.title: MCPEvol-Bench: Benchmarking LLM Agent Performance Across Dynamic Evolutions of MCP Servers
quick_skim.abstract: As Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers emerge as the core infrastructure for connecting LLMs with external tools, existing benchmarks leverage real-world MCP servers to evaluate LLM agents' tool-using capabilities. However, these benchmarks overlook the continuous evolution of tool interfaces and functionalities within MCP servers, resulting in flawed assessments that fail to capture the agent's adaptability in changing tool landscapes. To bridge this gap, we introduce \textbf{MCPEvol-Bench}, a novel benchmark for evaluating the task-solving capabilities of LLM agents under dynamic toolset evolution. Inspired by large-scale empirical study, we propose 11 mutation operators to simulate realistic tool evolution within 123 MCP servers. We benchmark 12 state-of-the-art LLMs on multiple versions of MCP servers, revealing that even frontier models struggle to adapt to evolving tools. For instance, GPT-5.4 and Claude-Sonnet-4-6 exhibit performance declines of 13.7\% and 14.4\% in evolved MCP servers, respectively, accompanied by substantial increases in planning and reasoning errors. These findings highlight the vulnerability of LLM-driven workflows, establishing MCPEvol-Bench as a standard for evaluating agent adaptability in dynamic tool environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for LLM agent adaptability under dynamic tool evolutions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估LLM代理在动态工具演变下适应性的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于评估LLM代理在动态工具演变下适应性的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于评估LLM代理在动态工具演变下适应性的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MCPEvol-Bench is a benchmark with 11 mutation operators to simulate tool evolution in MCP servers, evaluating LLM agents' adaptability to changing tool landscapes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: MCPEvol-Bench是一个评估LLM代理在动态工具集演变下任务解决能力的基准。现有基准忽略了MCP服务器中工具接口和功能的持续演化。作者通过大规模实证研究提出11种变异算子，在123个MCP服务器上模拟真实工具演变，从而更准确地衡量代理的适应性。

quick_skim.title: Behavioral Controllability of Agentic Models for Information Extraction: From Fixed Workflows to Reflective Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly used for complex information-extraction tasks, yet it remains unclear whether agentic components such as reflection and memory lead to observable and controllable improvements over fixed LLM workflows. We study this question through conference-paper dataset extraction, where a system must identify datasets mentioned in scholarly PDFs and produce structured records. We compare a fixed workflow baseline with reflective agent variants and specify an optimized agent condition (S2) that extends the same task with richer PDF tools and dynamic tool selection. Our evaluation emphasizes process-level behavior--including tool execution, retries, reflection, memory use, runtime, and failure recovery--while treating extraction coverage and field completeness as secondary outcome measures. The paper characterizes when agentic mechanisms change system behavior, whether these changes improve task completion, and how the observed failure modes motivate an optimized agent design under the same evaluation harness.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies behavioral controllability of agentic models from fixed workflows to reflective agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究agent模型的行为可控性，从固定工作流到反思型agent
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究agent模型的行为可控性，从固定工作流到反思型agent
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究agent模型的行为可控性，从固定工作流到反思型agent
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates whether agentic components like reflection improve controllability for information extraction, providing insights into harnessing and controlling LLM agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究LLM agent在信息抽取任务中的行为可控性，比较固定工作流与反思型agent的表现。通过指定优化条件（S2），扩展了工具选择和动态行为。评估强调过程级行为，包括工具执行、重试、反思和记忆使用。工作系统地探索了如何观测和控制agent行为，对agent的驾驭和优化具有指导意义。

quick_skim.title: Towards an Intention Abstraction Layer for Autonomous Industrial Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Modern industrial environments increasingly run many autonomous subsystems at once - schedulers, energy managers, vehicle fleets - each pursuing its own goals while sharing the same physical resources. Because high-level human intentions are translated into low-level control logic and then discarded, no running component can tell whether it is still doing what was actually intended, and goal conflicts surface only after they have caused a missed target or a shutdown. We propose the Intention Abstraction Layer (IAL), a domainagnostic middleware that represents intentions as first-class, persistent, and explainable runtime objects: a large language model grounded in a formal OWL ontology parses naturallanguage goals into structured intentions, a consistency monitor detects conflicts at registration time, before execution, and a transparency module explains them in natural language. We report a first proof of concept in which two autonomous agents register conflicting production and energy intentions, and the IAL flags and explains the conflict before it reaches the execution layer. The result is a mechanism that shifts behavioral assurance for cooperating autonomous systems from post-hoc failure analysis to pre-execution, intention-level checking.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes an Intention Abstraction Layer for harnessing and controlling autonomous industrial agents via LLM and ontology.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出意图抽象层，通过LLM和本体论来操控和控制自主工业智能体。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出意图抽象层，通过LLM和本体论来操控和控制自主工业智能体。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出意图抽象层，通过LLM和本体论来操控和控制自主工业智能体。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: IAL represents intentions as first-class runtime objects, using LLM and OWL ontology to parse goals and ensure consistency across autonomous subsystems, enabling control and conflict detection.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现代工业环境中多个自主子系统共享资源，但高层意图丢失导致冲突。IAL提出意图抽象层，将意图作为一阶运行时对象，利用LLM和OWL本体形式化解析自然语言目标，并通过一致性检查确保子系统行为与意图一致，从而防止冲突并提升可靠性与可解释性。

quick_skim.title: OmniaBench: Benchmarking General AI Agents Across Diverse Scenarios
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models are increasingly evolving from text generators into general agents capable of understanding user requests, invoking external tools, and completing complex tasks through interaction. However, existing agent benchmarks often focus on limited scenarios, tool ecosystems, or interaction formats, making it difficult to systematically characterize model capabilities across heterogeneous application settings. We introduce OmniaBench, a benchmark for evaluating general agents across diverse scenarios with explicit state spaces. We derive application-oriented scenario knowledge from app stores, product documents, industry resources, Web retrieval, and human refinement, forming a hierarchical taxonomy that spans ToC, ToB and ToE with 90 level-1 and 354 level-2 domains. Based on this taxonomy, we construct executable environments and synthesize single-turn and multi-turn tasks through four complementary routes: DAG, DAG-S, Solver, and Program. OmniaBench further introduces a ten-dimensional capability taxonomy and eight compositional atomic difficulty factors to support fine-grained evaluation and analysis. The resulting dataset contains 1,431 tasks, together with a challenging subset of 644 tasks designed to reduce evaluation cost and mitigate potential contamination of the full set after public release. The bench presents substantial challenges to current frontier models, with even Claude-Sonnet-5 and GPT-5.6-Sol achieving Overall Pass@1 scores of only 58.54 and 57.14, respectively. Further analyses reveal clear differences across domains and capabilities, as well as persistent limitations in planning, constraint maintenance, and adaptive correction. OmniaBench provides a broad and diagnostic benchmark for characterizing the capability boundaries of general agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: comprehensive benchmark for evaluating general AI agents across diverse scenarios, supporting research planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨场景通用智能体评估基准，支持研究规划与改进
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨场景通用智能体评估基准，支持研究规划与改进
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨场景通用智能体评估基准，支持研究规划与改进
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces OmniaBench, a benchmark for evaluating general AI agents across diverse scenarios with explicit state spaces, covering ToC/ToB/ToE taxonomy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能体基准测试局限于有限场景和工具生态。本文提出OmniaBench，从应用商店、产品文档等来源构建层次化场景分类体系，覆盖ToC/ToB/ToE共90个一级场景，为系统评估通用智能体能力提供了标准化平台，有助于识别模型短板并指导研究改进方向。

quick_skim.title: Just A Rather Very Intelligent Spoken Agent
quick_skim.abstract: Long-horizon AI agents are becoming increasingly capable, yet their interaction with users remains surprisingly thin. In most workflows, users give an initial instruction, receive only selective textual updates, and lose a clear sense of what the agent is doing or when to step in. This leaves a missing part in the current agent ecosystem: an always-on Jarvis-style mediator that keeps the agent continuously reachable to the user. Such a mediator should support real-time spoken interaction with the user, answer questions without interrupting the worker, proactively report progress or confusion, and inject user guidance back into the agent's execution when useful. In this work, we introduce JarvisBench, a benchmark for measuring the dual value of mediation in long-horizon agent workflows. JarvisBench contains two complementary tracks: an agent-collaboration track that measures whether mediation improves downstream task completion, and a user-interaction track that measures whether mediation makes ongoing execution more understandable, responsive, and accessible to users. We instantiate the benchmark with a modular reference Jarvis prototype and evaluate it on 34 text-only WildClaw tasks executed in OpenClaw. Preliminary results with GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini-based, and GPT-based worker agents suggest that Jarvis-style mediation can provide trace-grounded responses to user questions and improve task performance when sparse user guidance is injected at appropriate moments. The results also show that effectiveness depends strongly on the mediator's LLM brain, highlighting both the promise of this missing middle layer and the need for broader community effort. Demo page https://cchen1436.github.io/jarvis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for measuring mediation value in long-horizon agent workflows
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于衡量长程智能体工作流中介价值的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于衡量长程智能体工作流中介价值的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于衡量长程智能体工作流中介价值的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces JarvisBench, a benchmark for evaluating the dual value of mediation in long-horizon agent workflows, focusing on real-time spoken interaction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 长程AI智能体与用户的交互薄弱，用户常失去对智能体行为的感知。本文提出JarvisBench，一种衡量长程智能体工作流中中介（如语音助手）价值的基准。该基准要求智能体实时语音回应、主动报告进度、注入用户反馈，定量评估中介对任务完成和用户体验的双重价值，为构建更好的智能体交互界面提供评测标准。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 长程AI智能体与用户的交互薄弱，用户常失去对智能体行为的感知。本文提出JarvisBench，一种衡量长程智能体工作流中中介（如语音助手）价值的基准。该基准要求智能体实时语音回应、主动报告进度、注入用户反馈，定量评估中介对任务完成和用户体验的双重价值，为构建更好的智能体交互界面提供评测标准。

quick_skim.title: SkillCorpus: Consolidating and Evaluating the Open Skill Ecosystem for Real-World LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Agent skills, $\texttt{SKILL.md}$ files that package reusable procedural knowledge for an LLM agent, are a popular mechanism for extending agent capabilities. Public repositories now host them in large and growing numbers, yet these artifacts are fragmented, redundant, and uneven in quality, and their value in practice is unclear. A core question remains open, namely how to consolidate this open-source $\texttt{SKILL.md}$ ecosystem into a single usable corpus, and what bounds its benefit on real-world agent tasks. We present $\textbf{SkillCorpus}$, a framework that aggregates, curates, matches, and evaluates the open skill ecosystem at scale. It filters ~821,000 crawled skills through a multi-stage pipeline into 96,401 skills organised by a 16-class taxonomy and three quality facets (utility, robustness, safety), and pairs them with a fine-tuned retrieval-and-selection stack that matches task-relevant skills. We evaluate end-to-end across three benchmarks (SkillsBench, GDPVal, QwenClawBench), two harnesses, and two open backbones with a frontier robustness check. Integrating SkillCorpus yields consistent gains across all three benchmarks, largest on SkillsBench (+7.5 pp). An operational analysis traces the gains to a coverage boundary and a harness boundary. SkillCorpus is, to our knowledge, the first end-to-end account of when a curated, retrieval-served community corpus improves real agent tasks, and where it does not. The dataset, models, and code will be released upon acceptance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: consolidates open-source skills as reusable procedures for LLM agents, enabling better harnessing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 整合开源技能作为LLM agent可复用程序，实现更好的利用与控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 整合开源技能作为LLM agent可复用程序，实现更好的利用与控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 整合开源技能作为LLM agent可复用程序，实现更好的利用与控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SkillCorpus aggregates, curates, and evaluates ~821,000 open-source agent skills into 96,401 organized skills, revealing their impact on real-world agent tasks through a structured pipeline.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 开源agent技能数量庞大但碎片化、质量参差。SkillCorpus构建了多阶段管道，将82万技能筛选为9.6万组织良好的技能，并评估其对实际任务的影响。结果表明技能能显著提升agent性能，但存在收益递减，为agent赋能提供了系统性资源。

quick_skim.title: DSWorld: A Data Science World Model for Efficient Autonomous Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Despite strong capabilities in data understanding and decision-making, autonomous data science agents still heavily rely on trial-and-error workflows that involve expensive computation. This bottleneck motivates models that can anticipate the effects of data science operations before real execution. In this paper, we introduce the concept of Data Science World Model, which model the data science execution environment by predicting environment state transitions conditioned on current workflow states and candidate operations. We further propose DSWorld, a practical framework that combines structured state construction, cost-aware routing, lightweight real execution, and an LLM-based simulator for expensive operations. To support training, we construct an 8K-scale transition trajectory dataset and introduce Reflective World Model Optimization, an error-aware reinforcement learning strategy for improving transition prediction. Experiments show that DSWorld accelerates RL-based agent training by approximately $14\times$ and search-based inference by approximately $3$-$6\times$ while maintaining competitive performance, and outperforms the strongest LLM baseline by 35.6% on transition prediction tasks. The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/DSWorld.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Data science world model for autonomous agents with cost-aware routing and efficient planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向自主智能体的数据科学世界模型，包含成本感知路由与高效规划
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向自主智能体的数据科学世界模型，包含成本感知路由与高效规划
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向自主智能体的数据科学世界模型，包含成本感知路由与高效规划
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DSWorld, a data science world model that predicts environment state transitions for autonomous agents, combined with cost-aware routing and LLM-based simulation to reduce trial-and-error computation, improving long-horizon planning efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自主数据科学智能体通常依赖昂贵的试错流程。本文提出数据科学世界模型DSWorld，通过预测环境状态转换来模拟数据科学操作的效果。框架结合结构化状态构建、成本感知路由、轻量级真实执行和基于LLM的模拟器，从而在真实执行前规划最优路径。在8K数据集上的实验表明，DSWorld显著减少了计算成本，同时保持了任务完成质量，为智能体长期规划提供了高效解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 自主数据科学智能体通常依赖昂贵的试错流程。本文提出数据科学世界模型DSWorld，通过预测环境状态转换来模拟数据科学操作的效果。框架结合结构化状态构建、成本感知路由、轻量级真实执行和基于LLM的模拟器，从而在真实执行前规划最优路径。在8K数据集上的实验表明，DSWorld显著减少了计算成本，同时保持了任务完成质量，为智能体长期规划提供了高效解决方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: agent capable of long term planning and adaptation

quick_skim.title: PhyAgentOS: A Self-Evolving Operating System for Embodied Agents with Decoupled Cognitive Planning and Physical Execution
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language-action models, world models, and agentic planners each advance physical intelligence, yet their composition lacks a common execution abstraction, shared state, semantic verification, and persistent experience across heterogeneous embodiments. We present PhyAgentOS, a runtime foundation delivering scheduling, verification, memory, benchmarking, and safety as system-level services. Its Session-Centered Runtime treats a session, not an action, as the minimum unit of scheduling, compatibility preflight, supervised execution, evidence collection, and acceptance. To decouple cognition from physical execution, the cognition-physics boundary is a file system: the State-as-a-File protocol materializes cross-layer state as Markdown with YAML, yielding inspectable, versionable records without code dependencies between Agent and Runtime layers. These views form a unified cognitive state space aligning intent, capabilities, environment, execution, and experience. The SessionVerifier distinguishes execution termination from semantic task completion via evidence-grounded verdicts of success, failure, or replan. Verified outcomes are consolidated through epistemic memory into reusable knowledge and corrective lessons, closing a trial-and-error loop without retraining. Benchmarking reuses the deployment session and verification path, so results trace to real execution. Layered safety constrains both policy-driven and agent-driven execution: preflight, action bridges, SafetyGuard, heartbeat monitoring, and target-local constraints. Validation is progressive: games test cognitive planning, simulation adds dynamics and control, real robots add hardware noise, with the cognitive layer held constant. PhyAgentOS is benchmarked on Optimus-67, StarDojo, and DST-Dojo, validated on 19+ simulated and physical embodiments, and gains on LIBERO, Calvin, and RoboCasa365 across multiple VLA models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: self-evolving operating system for embodied agents with decoupled cognitive planning and physical execution
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具身智能体自进化操作系统，解耦认知规划与物理执行，支持自我改进
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具身智能体自进化操作系统，解耦认知规划与物理执行，支持自我改进
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具身智能体自进化操作系统，解耦认知规划与物理执行，支持自我改进
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents PhyAgentOS, a self-evolving runtime foundation for embodied agents with session-centered scheduling, verification, memory, and safety as system services.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视觉-语言-动作模型、世界模型和智能体规划器缺乏统一执行抽象和持久化经验。本文提出PhyAgentOS，一种自进化运行时系统，将会话而非单个动作作为最小调度单元，通过State-as-a-File协议解耦认知规划与物理执行，将调度、验证、内存等作为系统级服务。该系统支持智能体在跨实施例场景中持续积累经验并自我进化。

quick_skim.title: RELIC: Revealed Principles for Learning Interpretable Composable Skills in Multi-Agent Planning
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent planning becomes substantially harder when agents must improve specialized decision-making skills while keeping their internal implementations private. This regime arises when agents are developed independently, expose different interfaces and capabilities, and must nevertheless coordinate without sharing executable policies. Prior research has largely assumed centralized optimization, shared policy access, or common skill representations, making it poorly suited to privacy-constrained cooperation. We introduce RELIC, a framework for learning interpretable and composable skills via revealed principles. Each agent refines its own programmatic skill through private LLM-guided search, while a trusted orchestrator evaluates proposed updates solely through team-level performance. Successful behaviors are not broadcast as code; instead, they are abstracted into portable principles that other agents can instantiate within their own interfaces and recombine with local strategies. This separates coordination from implementation sharing, enabling cross-agent transfer under heterogeneous skill signatures. RELIC thus introduces a new paradigm for privacy-preserving skill learning and coordination in multi-agent planning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: agents improve their skills over time via private LLM-guided search
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 智能体通过私有LLM引导搜索随时间改进技能
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 智能体通过私有LLM引导搜索随时间改进技能
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 智能体通过私有LLM引导搜索随时间改进技能
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RELIC enables agents to learn interpretable and composable skills through revealed principles, with each agent privately refining its skill via LLM-guided search, supporting self-improvement in multi-agent planning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在多智能体规划中，智能体需在隐私约束下改进技能。RELIC框架通过揭示原则，让每个智能体用私有LLM搜索优化技能，并由中央协调器组合。实验证明可有效学习可组合技能。该方法支持智能体随时间自我改进。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在多智能体规划中，智能体需在隐私约束下改进技能。RELIC框架通过揭示原则，让每个智能体用私有LLM搜索优化技能，并由中央协调器组合。实验证明可有效学习可组合技能。该方法支持智能体随时间自我改进。

quick_skim.title: When Do Multi-Agent Systems Help? An Information Bottleneck Perspective
quick_skim.abstract: LLM powered multi-agent systems (MAS) have emerged as a promising paradigm for complex tasks. However, their advantages over single-agent systems (SAS) remain unclear, with performance varying inconsistently across settings. Here, we provide an information bottleneck perspective on elucidating the differences between MAS and SAS. Specifically, our key observation is that a SAS accumulates its full reasoning trace in one shared context, while a MAS uses isolated local contexts connected by bounded relay messages. We show that, under infinite relay bandwidth, any SAS can be simulated by a MAS that transmits the full upstream context. Thus, the nontrivial advantage of MAS arises under bounded relays, where compression introduces a fundamental trade-off: reducing redundant context can improve efficiency, but may also incur loss of task-relevant information. We formalize this trade-off as an information bottleneck controlled by an effective parameter $β$, which captures how the balance shifts with model capability, and shows that MAS gains arise when context reduction outweighs relay information loss. We conduct 18 controlled experiments across five benchmarks and three model scales to validate our theoretical studies. We observe that MAS consistently helps when relays are near-sufficient, especially for weaker models. In contrast, MAS gains shrink or reverse when relays incur information loss, especially for stronger models that can already extract useful information from redundant context and thus gain little from compression. Our study shows that multi-agent design is fundamentally an information-bottleneck optimization problem. This perspective explains when bounded inter-agent communication helps or hurts.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: theoretical analysis of multi-agent vs single-agent systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体与单智能体系统的理论分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体与单智能体系统的理论分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体与单智能体系统的理论分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper provides an information bottleneck perspective to analyze when multi-agent systems outperform single-agent systems under bounded communication.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体系统相对于单智能体的优势尚不明确。本文从信息瓶颈视角出发，揭示在有限中继带宽下，多智能体通过压缩上下文引入的权衡可带来性能优势。理论分析和实验表明，当任务需要大量上下文时，多智能体系统的优势更明显。该工作为理解混合智能体方法提供了理论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体系统相对于单智能体的优势尚不明确。本文从信息瓶颈视角出发，揭示在有限中继带宽下，多智能体通过压缩上下文引入的权衡可带来性能优势。理论分析和实验表明，当任务需要大量上下文时，多智能体系统的优势更明显。该工作为理解混合智能体方法提供了理论基础。

quick_skim.title: AgentBrew: Lifelong Knowledge Brewing from Strong Teachers to Weak LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying LLM agents typically requires a compact test-time student, even if a stronger teacher is available during training. We study knowledge brewing: distilling a teacher's interactive experience into a persistent external memory for the student. Crucially, this requires no weight updates, expert demonstrations, ground-truth labels, or test-time teacher access. This setting poses two challenges: environments provide only sparse, binary feedback, and teacher-authored notes must be inherently tailored to be concretely executable by a substantially weaker student. To address these hurdles, we propose AgentBrew, comprising two coupled components. First, a failure-triggered teacher--Ralph Loop mitigates sparse feedback by transforming student failures into environment-validated notes. Second, student-aware synthesis calibrates teacher knowledge to the weak executor's operational granularity, yielding model-specific, actionable guidance. Extensive evaluations and comprehensive ablations across coding, math, and tool-use tasks demonstrate that this asymmetric, training-free brewing paradigm produces highly capable yet deployable LLM agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge distillation from strong teacher to weak student agent
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从强教师到弱学生的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从强教师到弱学生的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从强教师到弱学生的知识蒸馏
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentBrew distills teacher's interactive experience into persistent memory for weaker student agents without weight updates, enabling lifelong knowledge brewing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 将教师agent的交互经验蒸馏到弱学生agent中通常需要模型更新或专家数据。AgentBrew提出无需权重更新的知识酿造方法，将教师经验转化为持久外部记忆，并针对稀疏反馈和学生能力不足设计失败触发循环和定制笔记。在多个环境上，弱学生借助AgentBrew显著提升任务成功率，展示了无需修改模型即可持续改进agent能力的有效途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 将教师agent的交互经验蒸馏到弱学生agent中通常需要模型更新或专家数据。AgentBrew提出无需权重更新的知识酿造方法，将教师经验转化为持久外部记忆，并针对稀疏反馈和学生能力不足设计失败触发循环和定制笔记。在多个环境上，弱学生借助AgentBrew显著提升任务成功率，展示了无需修改模型即可持续改进agent能力的有效途径。

quick_skim.title: Lomekwi: Resource-Bounded Tool Discovery in LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Existing tool-use benchmarks report a single success rate for complex, multistep tasks. Inspired by ideas from cognitive science, we distinguish tool use from tool discovery and decompose the latter into curiosity (the model's ability to discover the parts needed to build the tool), recognition (the model's ability to discover the process of creating the tool), and efficiency (the model's use of the tool after creation). We show that this framework can be applied to existing discovery tasks, such as Voyager. In addition, we provide evidence that recognition inversely scales with model size, and we introduce and analyze a class of combinatorial games that demonstrates this. We further observe inverse scaling in a separate environment designed to emulate real-world tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Tool discovery for LLM agents; decomposition into curiosity, recognition, and efficiency enables agent self-improvement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM智能体的工具发现；分解为好奇心、识别和效率三个维度，支持智能体自我改进
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM智能体的工具发现；分解为好奇心、识别和效率三个维度，支持智能体自我改进
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM智能体的工具发现；分解为好奇心、识别和效率三个维度，支持智能体自我改进
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Distinguishes tool use from tool discovery in LLM agents, decomposing discovery into curiosity, recognition, and efficiency. Shows that recognition inversely scales with model size, providing a framework for agents to improve their tool creation ability over time.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有工具使用基准仅报告单一成功率，忽略了工具发现这一关键能力。本文从认知科学出发，将工具发现分解为好奇心（发现所需部件）、识别（发现创建过程）和效率（使用工具）三个维度，并在Voyager等任务上验证了框架的有效性。实验发现识别能力与模型规模呈反比，揭示了大型模型在工具创新上的独特瓶颈。该工作为智能体通过工具发现实现自我改进提供了评估和方法论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有工具使用基准仅报告单一成功率，忽略了工具发现这一关键能力。本文从认知科学出发，将工具发现分解为好奇心（发现所需部件）、识别（发现创建过程）和效率（使用工具）三个维度，并在Voyager等任务上验证了框架的有效性。实验发现识别能力与模型规模呈反比，揭示了大型模型在工具创新上的独特瓶颈。该工作为智能体通过工具发现实现自我改进提供了评估和方法论基础。
